data.title: Citigroup said to expand Japan-based investment banking team on M&A boom
data.description:
Citigroup (C) is planning to expand its Japan-based investment banking team by about 30% by the first half of next year, thanks to a mergers-and-acquisitions boom in the country, according to a Tuesday media report.

The lender's Japan Vice Chair Masuo Fukuda declined to provide a headcount figure, Bloomberg reported.

On the back of corporate governance reforms, M&A deals in Japan are nearing $350B in 2025, the most since Bloomberg started gathering the data in 1998, even as the Bank of Japan hikes interest rates to multi-decade highs.

"The competition for talent is intensifying, requiring us to move fast with recruitment,” Fukuda said.

Citigroup (C) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: RxSight down on CFO transition
data.description:
• Shares of RxSight (RXST) lost ~13% on Tuesday after the ophthalmic medical device company announced that its longtime CFO, Shelley Thunen, is leaving the company.
  • Thumen, who joined RxSight (RXST) in 2017 and led the company’s finance function during its 2021 IPO, will remain in her role until a successor is appointed or January 31, 2026, whichever comes first.
  • Additionally, she has agreed to support the company as a consultant after the departure, RxSight (RXST) said in a statement on Monday.
  • “Shelley has been a key and trusted partner throughout some of the most important years in RxSight’s development,” added CEO Ron Kurtz. “She has shaped the foundation we rely on today, and we are truly grateful for everything she has done for our patients, our customers, and everyone at RxSight.”

data.title: SEI Investments upped to Overweight at Piper as sales wins accumulate
data.description:
SEI Investments (SEIC) stock rose 2.7% in Tuesday morning trading after Piper Sandler upgraded the investment firm's rating to Overweight from Neutral as SEIC stands to capitalize from accelerating deal growth in the alternatives landscape into 2026.

Analyst Crispin Love points to $144M in sales wins for the firm over the past year, up 40% Y/Y. "As these wins onboard over coming quarters, we expect meaningful revenue acceleration and margin expansion," he wrote in a note to clients. "SEIC already operates at nearly 30% margins, but this sales inflection combined with continued expense discipline should drive further upside."

Love cites its alternatives outsourcing opportunity as its primary growth engine. "With marquee clients including Blackstone, Blue Owl, and Carlyle. SEIC has significant runway to both deepen these existing relationships and capture new logos in this expanding market," the analyst said.

Furthermore, Piper believes the market underappreciates the capital return prospects. SEI (SEIC) targets returning more than 90% of free cash flow to shareholders through dividends and buybacks.

PIper's Overweight rating on SEI (SEIC) contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Hold and agrees with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: Genentech's subcutaneous Lunsumio gains FDA approval
data.description:
• The US FDA has approved a subcutaneous version of Roche (RHHBY)-owned Genentech's follicular lymphoma treatment, Lunsumio (mosunetuzumab).
  • Lunsumio VELO takes about one minute to administer, compared to roughly two to four hours with the infusion version.
  • Lunsumio VELO received accelerated approval based on results of the phase I/II GO29781 study that found an objective response rate and complete response rate in patients with third-line or later follicular lymphoma given the treatment of 75% and 59%, respectively.

data.title: One of the biggest comeback stories in the retail sector is a secondhand clothes seller
data.description:
ThredUp (TDUP) has seen an explosive rally in 2025 as investors have repriced the stock from being a distressed, money-losing niche platform to a viable, high-growth, asset-light resale infrastructure play.

Fueled by new shoppers and increased order growth, ThredUp (TDUP) experienced its fourth consecutive quarter of accelerating revenue growth in Q3, exceeding Wall Street’s expectations and driving shares more than 7% higher in Monday’s after-hours trading.

For the holiday quarter, ThredUp's (TDUP) total revenue is expected to be up 14% to a range of $76.0M to $78.0M. Gross margin is projected to be in the range of 78.0% to 79.0% with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 3.0%, compared to 80.4% and 4.1% in the same quarter last year. ThredUp (TDUP) appears to be on the verge of posting its first profitable quarter.

ThredUp (TDUP) operates an e-commerce platform where consumers buy and sell secondhand women’s and kids’ clothing, shoes, and accessories at significant discounts to original retail prices. Notably, ThredUp (TDUP) positions itself between traditional thrift and peer-to-peer resale marketplaces by owning more of the value chain, including logistics, pricing, merchandising, and customer service.

Shares of ThredUp (TDUP) are up just under 400% in 2025 to rank near the very top of the retail sector. Short interest on TDUP stands at 15.8% of the total float. The market cap is just over $912 million.

data.title: Top Quant rated copper stocks as the metal breaches $12,000 for the first time
data.description:
Copper prices reached a new high of over $12,000 per metric ton due to supply issues at major mines and significant changes in global trade linked to U.S. tariffs under President Trump.

This increase is set to make it the best year for copper since the 2009 financial crisis.

In light of this development, below is a list of top copper stocks ranked according to their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings.

The list is topped by First Quantum Minerals (FQVLF) and Southern Copper (SCCO), both earning Strong Buy Quant Ratings of 4.76. Ero Copper (ERO) follows closely behind with a Strong Buy rating of 4.54. Lundin Mining (LUNMF), Taseko Mines (TGB) and Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) round out the list with Hold ratings.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant system grades stocks based on their relative performance across various critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any rating of 3.5 or above considered a bullish rating and any rating of 2.5 or below considered a bearish rating.

Here is the list:

  1. First Quantum Minerals (FQVLF), Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.76)

  2. Southern Copper (SCCO), Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.76)

  3. Ero Copper (ERO), Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.54)

  4. Lundin Mining (LUNMF), Quant Rating: Hold (3.47)

  5. Taseko Mines (TGB), Quant Rating: Hold (3.23)

  6. Freeport-McMoRan (FCX), Quant Rating: Hold (3.06)

Copper and Copper Mining ETFs: (CPER), (COPX), and (JJCTF).

data.title: Jim Beam to idle Kentucky distillery as bourbon demand slows
data.description:
Jim Beam will suspend production at its Clermont, Kentucky, distillery for a year as sluggish spirits sales, costly import tariffs, and changing consumer tastes weigh on the world’s largest bourbon maker.

While the company said it will use this time to invest in “site enhancements,” it acknowledged that demand has not met supply.

“We are always assessing production levels to best meet consumer demand and recently met with our team to discuss our volumes for 2026,” the company said in a statement.

Jim Beam is owned by Suntory Global Spirits, an entity of Japan’s Suntory Group.

The closure – albeit temporarily – underscores the challenges faced by the spirits industry. A recent Gallup poll found that the number of adults who consume alcohol has dropped to a 90-year low, while an existing ban on U.S. spirit sales in Canada drove exports of American-made bourbon into Canada down 85%.

Exports to the UK and Japan each declined by more than 23%, while exports to the EU were down 12%. Canada, Japan, the EU and the UK accounted for 70% of all U.S. spirits exported in 2024. And as American whiskey inventory has tripled since 2012, exports have been critical to the industry to compensate for declining domestic sales.

“Total distilled spirits has weakened in the last 6 months or so, not dramatically, but it has,” said Brown-Forman CEO Lawson Whiting on the company’s most recent  earnings call responding to a question about holiday sales. “Our expectations for this holiday season are—we're trying to be optimistic, but at the same time, we are looking and seeing some of the macro trends that are tough."

Brown-Foreman makes Jack Daniel’s, Woodford Reserve, and Old Forester bourbons.

While Diageo -- parent company of Johnnie Walker whiskey -- lowered FY26 sales forecasts to compensate for a weaker U.S. consumer environment “than originally planned for.”

“We are not satisfied with our current performance and are focused on what we can manage and control; acting with speed to drive efficiencies, prioritizing investment and adapting more quickly to an evolving consumer environment,” Diageo interim CEO Nik Jhangiani said.

For bourbon drinkers, the decision to trim new production at Jim Beam will not impact future supply with the Kentucky distilleries currently aging a record 16.1 million barrels of bourbon, the Kentucky Distillers’ Association says, up 27% from last year and the highest level since the repeal of Prohibition in 1933.

Shares of Brown-Foreman (BF-A) (BF-B), and Diageo (DEO) are both lower on Tuesday.

data.title: Hycroft among top S&P gainers after higher-grade Vortex silver report
data.description:
• Hycroft Mining Holding (HYMC) jumped 5.6% Tuesday morning, ranking among the S&P’s top gainers, following a 49% surge in the previous session
  • The company announced drill results from the 2025-2026 exploration drill program on Monday at the Hycroft Mine, located in Nevada, USA, a tier-1 mining jurisdiction. Reports the highest grades to date identified in Vortex.
  • Vortex expands approximately 70 meters to the northwest and approximately 90 meters down-dip to the west based on results from H25D-6070 and H25D-6072.
  • Hycroft (HYMC) shares have more than doubled over the past month, gaining 75% in the last five days alone.

data.title: Pfizer reports patient death in hemophilia trial for Hympavzi
data.description:
• Pfizer (PFE) on Tuesday announced that a patient who was taking part in a long-term extension trial for its hemophilia therapy Hympavzi (marstacimab) died after serious adverse events.
  • The patient died in December following serious adverse events of cerebellar infarction and subsequent cerebral hemorrhage, Pfizer (PFE) said, adding that further investigations are ongoing to understand the events that led to his death.
  • In 2024, the U.S. FDA approved Hympavzi as a once-weekly subcutaneous prophylactic treatment to prevent or reduce the frequency of bleeding episodes in people with hemophilia A or B.
  • The New York-based company was evaluating the drug’s long-term safety and efficacy in hemophilia patients who had completed its BASIS and BASIS KIDS Phase 3 trials for marstacimab. The patient in question had completed the BASIS trial before entering the long-term extension study in 2023, according to Pfizer (PFE).

data.title: Booking Holdings is seeing improved web traffic in Q4
data.description:
Booking Holdings (BKNG) is seeing momentum in the fourth quarter, according to a data dive by Wedbush Securities.

Analyst Scott Devitt and his team pointed to positive quarter-to-date trends through the end of November relative to expectations. The analyst team noted that web traffic to Booking on a consolidated level grew +8% year-over-year QTD, which was an acceleration of about 600 basis points from the third-quarter trend. "Booking app MAU growth accelerated ~100 bps from 3Q, primarily supported by a sequential improvement across Booking.com, Agoda, and Kayak. We believe web and app engagement trends are directionally encouraging versus expectations, which currently call for a ~220 bps deceleration in room night growth to +5.9% Y/Y (from +8.0% in 3Q)," highlighted Devitt.

Shares of Booking Holdings (BKNG) edged 0.1% higher in Tuesday morning trading to $5,410.61 vs. the 52-week range of $4,096.23 to $5,839.41.
